decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of ~43.2°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. The current value is at the upper bound (500.0), but the editable variable definition allows increasing it (RecommendedDirection=increase) and the bounds listed in the prompt text imply potential for adjustment or re-evaluation of the limit. However, strictly adhering to the provided bounds [20.0, 500.0], the conductivity is already maxed out. Therefore, I will pivot to the next most effective lever: increasing the heat spreader width to enhance lateral spreading area, which is currently 0.9m and can be increased up to 1.0m.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width maximizes the lateral area available for heat dissipation towards the Dirichlet boundaries, helping to lower the peak chip temperature further.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the lateral direction
risk_notes: Approaching the design domain width limit (1.2m); ensure the spreader does not overlap with boundary condition application points incorrectly if logic changes
Diminishing returns as the spreader approaches the full domain width
